Overview

Address
151 GOODVIEW DRIVE, APOLLO, PA 15613
Phone
4127273451

177
Avg daily residents
140 (79% of beds filled)

For-profit LLC
Provider type

Medicare/Medicaid since
1976-12-03
Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.37 3.78 3.44 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.39 0.66 0.40 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.93 0.84 0.95 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.05 2.28 2.09 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.32 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.04 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.03
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.31
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.09
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
62.2%
annual
RN turnover
72.4%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.34
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.97
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-05-22 35 25 13 291 1 291
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-08-23 27 17 10 132 2 198
Total weighted health score
267.8
lower is better

Deficiencies (74)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

71 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
2 G/H/I Actual harm
1 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. G 2026-01-06 2026-01-30
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. J 2025-11-13 2025-12-16
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. D 2025-11-13 2025-12-16
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-11-13 2025-12-16
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-09-09 2025-09-24
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. D 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0628 Provide the required documentation or notification related to the resident's needs, appeal rights, or bed-hold policies. E 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. E 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0638 Assure that each resident’s assessment is updated at least once every 3 months. D 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. E 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0676 Ensure residents do not lose the ability to perform activities of daily living unless there is a medical reason. D 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. E 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0699 Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. E 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0711 Ensure the resident's doctor reviews the resident's care, writes, signs and dates progress notes and orders, at each required visit. D 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. D 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0849 Arrange for the provision of hospice services or assist the resident in transferring to a facility that will arrange for the provision of hospice services. D 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. F 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. G 2025-05-22 2025-06-16
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. D 2025-05-22 2025-07-03
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-04-15 2025-05-01
0575 Post a list of names, addresses, and telephone numbers of all pertinent State agencies and advocacy groups and a statement that the resident may file a complaint with the State Survey Agency. D 2025-03-18 2025-04-21
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2025-03-18 2025-04-21
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2025-03-18 2025-04-21
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-03-18 2025-04-21
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-12-27 2025-02-17
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-12-27 2025-02-17
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2024-08-23 2024-10-11
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2024-08-23 2024-10-11
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. D 2024-08-23 2024-10-11
0622 Not transfer or discharge a resident without an adequate reason; and must provide documentation and convey specific information when a resident is transferred or discharged. E 2024-08-23 2024-10-11
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. E 2024-08-23 2024-10-11
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. E 2024-08-23 2024-10-11
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2024-08-23 2024-10-11
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. E 2024-08-23 2024-10-11
0691 Provide appropriate colostomy, urostomy, or ileostomy care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2024-08-23 2024-10-11
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2024-08-23 2024-10-11
0699 Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. E 2024-08-23 2024-10-11
0742 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with mental disorder or psychosocial adjustment difficulty, or who has a history of trauma and/or post-traumatic stress disorder. D 2024-08-23 2024-10-11
0744 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with dementia. D 2024-08-23 2024-10-11
